title: Parkio Daily for July 23, 2026
teaser: Happily Ever After and Fantasmic showtimes shift late August; Six Ravens opens soon at Disney Springs; DVC Welcome Home Weeks start today with 40% dining deals.

rides.name: Space Mountain
rides.note: First-hour windows often stay under 20 minutes

rides.name: Tower of Terror
rides.note: Rope drop keeps waits shortest before 10am

breaking.title: Six Ravens by Gideon's opening very soon at Disney Springs
breaking.body: The new signature restaurant is set to open today or in the coming days at Disney Springs. The full menu includes both food and drink options from the team behind Gideon's Bakehouse.

parkioInsight.text: First-day crowds at new restaurants tend to peak mid-afternoon and evening. Aiming for a late lunch window around 2–3pm often yields shorter waits and easier walk-up seating.

breaking.title: DVC Welcome Home Weeks begin today with 40% dining discounts
breaking.body: Disney Vacation Club members can now claim resort perks, restaurant discounts of up to 40%, and exclusive freebies through the summer and into fall as part of Welcome Home Weeks.

parkioInsight.text: Member-exclusive dining windows at fan-favorite spots like 'Ohana tend to fill quickly once announced. Booking early in the promotion period usually improves availability for hard-to-get reservations.

bignews.title: Happily Ever After and Fantasmic showtimes change in late August
bignews.body: Both Magic Kingdom and Hollywood Studios nighttime spectaculars will shift to new schedules starting late August. Exact dates and times have been posted to the official calendar.

parkioInsight.text: Schedule shifts often create lighter crowds for the first few nights as guests adjust. Catching a show early in the new window may yield better viewing spots with less pre-show congestion.

bignews.title: Kraft Heinz partnership announced for Disney parks and cruise line
bignews.body: Disney has signed a major sponsorship deal with Kraft Heinz, starting at the D23 Expo in August. The alliance will extend across Walt Disney World, Disneyland, and Disney Cruise Line locations.

parkioInsight.text: New corporate sponsorships often bring limited-time menu items and promotional giveaways in the first weeks. Checking quick-service menus at EPCOT and Magic Kingdom early in August may surface exclusive offerings before they rotate out.

bignews.title: Connections Eatery at EPCOT switches from plates to paper boats
bignews.body: The quick-service restaurant has replaced reusable plates with disposable paper food boats for most menu items. The change went into effect this week.

parkioInsight.text: Paper boats tend to make sharing platters and hot items messier, especially for families. If you plan to split meals or order heavier entrees, bringing your own napkins or choosing a table-service spot nearby may improve the experience.

bignews.title: New EPCOT Food and Wine Festival signature events announced
bignews.body: A fresh series of premium culinary experiences will debut during this fall's festival, adding to the lineup of tastings, chef demonstrations, and exclusive dinners already planned for the season.

parkioInsight.text: Premium festival events often sell out within days of booking windows opening. Watching for reservation releases in early August and booking immediately tends to secure spots for the most popular chef-led sessions.

topstories.title: Bayou Bar chocolate inspired by Dubai trend debuts at Disneyland
topstories.body: The new Princess and the Frog–themed candy bar features milk chocolate filled with pistachio cream, following the viral Dubai chocolate trend. It is priced at $6.49.

parkioInsight.text: Viral food items at Disneyland typically sell out at flagship candy shops by early afternoon. Stopping at Main Street Confectionery or Pooh Corner before 11am usually improves your odds of snagging one.

topstories.title: Sally Nightmare Before Christmas ear headband and pin arrive at Disneyland
topstories.body: A new sequined ear headband featuring Sally and a matching pin are now available at select Disneyland Resort merchandise locations ahead of the Halloween season.

parkioInsight.text: Seasonal headbands tied to Nightmare Before Christmas often move quickly once Halloween merchandise drops. Checking Emporium or World of Disney early in the day tends to yield the best size and color selection.

topstories.title: Mickey Family Halloween collection launches July 27 online and in parks
topstories.body: A new pumpkin-themed line starring Mickey and Friends will debut on DisneyStore.com July 27, with select items arriving at Disney Parks shortly after. The collection includes apparel, accessories, and home goods.

parkioInsight.text: Online-first merchandise drops often sell out popular sizes within the first 24 hours. Setting a reminder for the 27th and shopping early morning Pacific time usually improves availability before in-park stock arrives.

topstories.title: Winnie the Pooh tea collection now at Walt Disney World
topstories.body: A new line of themed tea tins from the fictional 100 Acre Wood Tea Company has arrived in select shops. Each tin features different Pooh characters and floral designs.

parkioInsight.text: Specialty food items like tea collections tend to cluster at EPCOT's UK pavilion and Magic Kingdom's Emporium. If hunting the full set, checking both locations mid-morning often surfaces the widest variety before stock rotates.

topstories.title: Magic Kingdom closes at 6pm August 7 for Halloween party
topstories.body: Guests without a separate Halloween party ticket will need to exit Magic Kingdom by 6pm on August 7 as Mickey's Not-So-Scary Halloween Party begins for the season. Lightning Lane availability also drops for daytime guests that evening.

parkioInsight.text: Early close dates typically see heavier morning crowds as day guests compress touring into fewer hours. Arriving at rope drop and tackling headliners first usually keeps waits manageable before the mid-afternoon surge.

topstories.title: Haunted Mansion rubber duck set launches on Disney Store online
topstories.body: A new collectible set of Haunted Mansion–themed rubber ducks is now available on DisneyStore.com. The set includes multiple character designs inspired by the attraction.

parkioInsight.text: Limited-edition Haunted Mansion collectibles often restock sporadically once the first batch sells out. Adding the item to your cart and checking back daily tends to catch restocks before they vanish again.

topstories.title: Disney launches .YNSD clothing brand, debuts at D23 Expo
topstories.body: Walt Disney Imagineering has unveiled a new apparel brand called Disney .YNSD, designed to bring Disney stories into everyday fashion. The line will debut at the D23 Expo in August before wider release.

parkioInsight.text: D23-exclusive merchandise often commands higher resale prices once the event ends. If attending the expo, prioritizing the Imagineering booth early on Day 1 usually secures limited-edition pieces before stock runs low.

icymi.title: Disney Worldbuilders documentary coming to Disney+ in August
icymi.body: A new behind-the-scenes series exploring the creation of immersive Disney park environments will premiere on Disney+ next month. The show covers projects from Zootopia to Star Wars lands.

icymi.title: Mickey Main Street fire truck toy now at Walt Disney World
icymi.body: A new toy replica of the iconic Main Street fire truck, featuring Mickey Mouse, is available at select Walt Disney World shops and on DisneyStore.com for $34.99.

icymi.title: Disney Parks Blog spotlights five chefs across Walt Disney World
icymi.body: A new feature highlights five culinary leaders bringing their personal backgrounds and cultures into the kitchens of Walt Disney World resort restaurants.

icymi.title: Disneyland Paris shares full tour video of Disney Adventure World
icymi.body: Disneyland Paris has released a complete flythrough tour of Disney Adventure World, the newly renamed and reimagined park formerly known as Walt Disney Studios Park.

spotlight.title: Kids' meal guide for Disneyland Resort summer visitors
spotlight.body: Disney Parks Blog has published a comprehensive roundup of every Disneyland Resort location offering kids' meals, complete with tips for saving money and maximizing value during the summer season. The guide covers both table-service and quick-service spots across Disneyland and California Adventure.

parkioInsight.text: Kids' meal portions at Disneyland often work well for light adult eaters, especially at quick-service counters. Ordering a kids' entree plus a side can keep costs lower while avoiding food waste during midday heat.
